description_long: This is a single-propeller, low-wing monoplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic WWII-era design. The airplane features a predominantly white body with red circular insignias on the wings and tail, and a red accent stripe running along the fuselage. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned just behind the propeller and ahead of the wing root. The landing gear is deployed, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all accented in red. The propeller is a three-blade design with a metallic spinner at the nose.
description: A white, low-wing monoplane with red markings, a single propeller, and an enclosed cockpit. It has deployed landing gear and a classic WWII-era appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, red accent stripe, cockpit canopy, and deployed landing gear.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the three-blade propeller, symmetrical wings with red roundels, and the landing gear alignment.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail section, red accents, and the full wing profile with visible landing gear.
description_view_3: Head-on view emphasizing the propeller, cockpit canopy, and the broad wingspan with red roundels.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing monoplane with straight wings and no visible winglets; slight dihedral angle present.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy just behind the propeller.
